findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 32. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Spurious thumbnail images of table data embedded within Table A2 in the HTML that do not exist in the PDF. Small, largely illegible embedded images of table portions appear between text-converted table rows near the K10/K11 boundary and the K38/K39 boundary.
findings.evidence: PDF pages 7 and 8 contain only clean text-based tables for Table A2. The HTML rendering inserts small thumbnail images (appearing to be captured portions of the PDF table) between rows K10 and K11, and between rows K38 and K39. These images are not present in the source PDF.

findings.description: Reading order of Table A2 is disrupted by spurious embedded thumbnail images inserted between table rows, forcing a reader to skip over illegible image artifacts to continue reading sequential table data.
findings.evidence: In the HTML, Table A2 rows K1-K10 are followed by an embedded image thumbnail, then K11-K38; similarly K28-K38 are followed by another embedded image thumbnail, then K39-K62. The PDF presents all rows sequentially without interruption.

findings.description: Embedded page-screenshot images inserted as table rows within Table A1 and Table A2, corrupting the table structure. In Table A1, two spurious rows appear after K24 and after K51, each containing an in the first and nine empty cells. In Table A2, two spurious rows appear after K10 and after K38, each containing an in the first and two empty cells. All 64 data rows in each table are present, but the structure is broken by these artifact rows.
findings.evidence: HTML Table A1 after K24: .... Same pattern after K51. HTML Table A2 after K10 and K38 have identical artifact rows with alt="Table A2 continued".

findings.description: Five page-screenshot images are embedded in the HTML alongside the fully extracted HTML table data, creating duplicate representations of the same content. The PDF contains no standalone images; these are conversion artifacts that reproduce table content already present as text.
findings.evidence: Image 1: appears immediately before the containing the same data as HTML rows. Images 2-5 appear inside Table A1 (after K24, after K51) and Table A2 (after K10, after K38) with alt text "Table A1 continued" / "Table A2 continued", duplicating data already rendered as table rows.
